item.title: Portfolio
item.description: This page is a centralized listing of my professional work and will be updated as time goes on. In addition, you can find my resume here. Junlab (Apr 2019 – May 2020) At the Jun Laboratory, I had the opportunity to build a “virtual twin” of the lab’s current robotic setup in VR. Along the way, I had the opportunity to expand the virtual twin into a more ambitious project, NERVV: an open-source, easily-integratable C# framework to visualize and control material-production pipelines in virtual reality with Unity 3D and ROS.

item.title: Free Software Hall of Fame
item.description: As you might know, I’m an avid free software advocate, and I try to use free software over proprietary ones as much as I reasonably can. These are some of the best free software projects that I have found, which I either use on a daily basis or have made me say “how did I possibly live before this?”. Hopefully you’ll be able to discover a project that’ll make you feel the same, too!

item.title: Envision Center projects
item.description: I worked at the Purdue Envision Center from January 2019 to August 2020. During my time there, I had the opportunity to work on some really interesting and rewarding projects. Most of these projects were created with C#/.NET using Unity 3D, and range anywhere from roughly 2k to over 10k lines of code. Almost all of these projects are public and able to be used in a browser, so definitely don’t hesitate to try some of them out!
